summary: Improved fighter ft_0852.c decomp accuracy by adding definitions and declarations for ft_800852B0, ft_8008549C, gFtDataList, ft_8045993C, and ft_8045996C; moving shared extern/type information into ft_0852.h; and refining UnkCostumeStruct layout in types.h. The PR body reports ft_8008549C matched 100% and ft_800852B0 improved from 0% to 87%, with remaining issues attributed to .bss label/section handling.
what_changed: Removed placeholder include usage from ft_0852.c and added fighter-specific includes, including ft/inlines.h, ft/types.h, and ft/forward.h.
Defined ftData* gFtDataList[FTKIND_MAX], ft_8045993C_t ft_8045993C[6], and int ft_8045996C[FTKIND_MAX] in ft_0852.c instead of leaving related declarations scattered elsewhere.

Implemented ft_800852B0 as a reset/initialization routine over CostumeListsForeachCharacter, ftData_Table_Unk0, and ftData_UnkIntPairs, clearing gFtDataList entries, costume joint pointers, costume pad_x8 fields, and count-struct data pointers.
Added an inline helper ft_800852B0_Reset_ft_8045993C(int i) to clear fields of ft_8045993C_t, then invoked it explicitly for indices 0 through 5.
Implemented ft_8008549C as a loop clearing ft_8045996C entries for indices 0 through 32.
Moved ft_8045993C_t from an ad hoc extern struct declaration in ftdata.c into ft_0852.h, expanding the layout from a six-byte pad plus one bitfield to named padding and bitfields at offset +6.
Added extern declarations in ft_0852.h for gFtDataList, ft_8045993C, ft_8045996C, CostumeListsForeachCharacter, ftData_Table_Unk0, and ftData_UnkIntPairs.
Removed duplicate or now-obsolete extern declarations from fighter.c and ftdata.c.
Refined UnkCostumeStruct in types.h by replacing a 0x0C padding blob with explicit fields pad_x8, pad_xC, and pad_x10 between x4 and x14_archive.
smart_moves: Centralized shared data declarations in ft_0852.h, reducing duplicate extern struct definitions and making ft_8045993C visible with one consistent type.

Used pointer-walking loop variables for CostumeListsForeachCharacter, ftData_Table_Unk0, and ftData_UnkIntPairs in ft_800852B0, matching the likely generated code shape better than repeated indexed global accesses.
Split ft_8045993C clearing into an inline helper, likely to reproduce repeated store patterns while keeping the C source readable.
Made the unknown costume struct padding more granular, exposing pad_x8 as a likely pointer because ft_800852B0 explicitly assigns NULL to it.
Kept uncertainty comments where evidence was incomplete, such as bitfield layout concerns and the unknown type of pad_x8, rather than over-naming unsupported fields.
decomp_lessons: When a function is blocked by data-section or .bss symbol issues, matching can still improve substantially by defining the involved globals in the most natural owning translation unit and consulting project data-section rules.

Unknown padding should be split into typed fields only when code behavior gives evidence; here assignment of NULL to offset +8 justified naming pad_x8 separately and treating it as pointer-like, though its exact type remained unknown.
Ad hoc extern struct definitions inside .c files should be promoted to headers once multiple fighter files need the same symbol/type, especially for globals like ft_8045993C.
For reset/init functions over arrays of structs, pointer increment loops can be a useful matching tactic when indexed forms fail to reproduce compiler output.
Bitfields are fragile for matching and layout; comments documenting that they are suspected to be off are useful future breadcrumbs.
naming_conventions: Unknown global data retained address-based names: ft_8045993C and ft_8045996C.
The struct type for address-named data used the project style ft_8045993C_t.
Unknown fields used offset-based names such as pad_x0, pad_x4, pad_x8, pad_xC, pad_x10, x6_b0, and x6_b1_b2.
Existing data-table names followed ftData_* style: ftData_Table_Unk0 and ftData_UnkIntPairs.
Known fighter constants and types retained project capitalization: FTKIND_MAX, FighterKind, Fighter_GObj, HSD_GObj, HSD_Joint, HSD_MatAnimJoint, and HSD_Archive.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reported match status: ft_800852B0 improved from 0% to 87%.
Used explicit unrolled calls to ft_800852B0_Reset_ft_8045993C for indices 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 instead of a loop, likely to better match emitted code.
Used pointer induction variables var_r8, var_r9, and var_r10 over adjacent fighter data arrays while maintaining integer loop index i.
Declared gFtDataList, ft_8045993C, and ft_8045996C in ft_0852.c, addressing symbol ownership/section placement as part of the matching attempt.
Remaining mismatch was attributed by the author to .bss label issues; reviewer pointed to the repository Data-sections wiki for rules on symbol section determination.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Provided the project wiki link for Data-sections and explained that it contains the rules for how symbol sections are determined, in response to the author's .bss label issue.

review_feedback.author: rbasamoyai
review_feedback.text: Noted that ft_800852B0 still had .bss label issues and asked for help fixing them.

follow_up_queries: Look up the repository Data-sections wiki before moving or defining globals whose section placement affects matching.
Search later PRs for ft_800852B0 to see whether the remaining 13% mismatch and .bss label issue were resolved.
Search later PRs for ft_8045993C_t or ft_8045993C to see whether the bitfield layout was renamed or corrected.
Search later PRs for UnkCostumeStruct pad_x8 to determine the real pointer type of the field cleared in ft_800852B0.
Check whether ft_8045996C length should use FTKIND_MAX consistently instead of a literal 33 in ft_8008549C.
